etymology.description: 消(消) (xiāo, "to disappear; to eliminate") + 息(息) (xī, "rest; interest; news")
etymology.interpretation: The word 消息(消息) (xiāoxi) combines 消(消) (xiāo, "to disappear; to eliminate") with 息(息) (xī, "rest; interest; news") to imply the concept of news or information as something that comes and goes, flows, or spreads, much like the elimination of silence with the arrival of sound or news.

examples.chinese: 我收到了一条好消息(我收到了一條好消息)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ shōudào le yī tiáo hǎo xiāoxi.
examples.english: I received a piece of good news.

examples.chinese: 他打电话告诉我一个重要的消息(他打電話告訴我一個重要的消息)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dǎ diànhuà gàosù wǒ yī gè zhòngyào de xiāoxi.
examples.english: He called to tell me an important piece of information.

examples.chinese: 这条消息引起了广泛的关注(這條消息引起了廣泛的關注)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo xiāoxi yǐnqǐ le guǎngfàn de guānzhù.
